symbols: triangular wing-like form — the angelic nature abstracted to its essential geometry
round face with minimal features — the otherworldly simplicity of heavenly beings
red heart-shaped form — the divine love that animates angelic service
description: An abstract drawing of a figure composed of simple geometric shapes. A large pink-lavender circular head with minimal line-drawn features (eyes, mouth) sits atop a triangular golden-ochre body suggesting a wing or robe. Within the triangle, a red shape with a curved interior motif suggests a heart or emblem. The background is pale blue. The style uses rough crayon or pastel strokes with visible texture and childlike simplicity.
theological_description: Klee's abstract angel reflects on the nature of heavenly beings through radical simplification. Angels in Scripture are messengers of God — the Greek angelos means 'messenger' — sent to announce, protect, and serve. The 'still feminine' quality hints at the tradition that angels, though incorporeal spirits, can manifest in ways that evoke tenderness and nurture. The Psalmist declares: 'He will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways' (Psalm 91:11). The abstract rendering strips the angel of Renaissance wings and flowing robes to suggest something more primal — the bare essence of a being whose existence is oriented entirely toward God. Hebrews asks: 'Are not all angels ministering spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation?' (Hebrews 1:14).

scripture_refs.reason: God commands his angels to guard — the protective angelic function
Angels as ministering spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation
